AWM Accounting System — v3.8.0

Paano Mag-Start ng New Month?

Step-by-Step Manual for Month-End Processing
Prepared by AWM Development Team • April 2026

What Does "Starting a New Month" Mean?

In the AWM Accounting System, "starting a new month" means you are closing the current accounting period and rolling forward all your account balances into the next month. This is called Month-End Processing.

Think of it like flipping a page in a notebook — everything you've written (your transactions) gets summarized and carried over, and you get a fresh page for the next month.

💡 Key ConceptYou don't "open" a new month. You close the current one, and the system automatically moves you to the next month.

Before You Begin (Checklist)

Before you can close the month, make sure all of these are done:

#TaskWhere to Check
1All transactions for the month have been enteredFS → Main tab → Data Entry
2All transactions have been posted (unposted count = zero)FS → Main tab → Post All Transactions
3Review your reports — check Trial Balance, Income StatementFS → Query/Report tab
4Back up your database (strongly recommended!)FS → File tab → Backup Database
⚠️ ImportantIf there are still unposted transactions, the system will block you from closing the month. You must post them first.

Step-by-Step Guide: How to Close the Month

1
Log in and select your company

Open the AWM Accounting System. Enter your username and password. After logging in, choose Financial Statements, then select the company you want to work on.

Login → System Options → Financial Statements → Select Company

Screenshot: Login Page
Screenshot: System Options — Choose "Financial Statements"
Screenshot: Select Company
2
Check the FS Main Menu

After selecting a company, you'll land on the Financial Statements main screen. Here you can see the current period and the number of unposted transactions.

Screenshot: Financial Statements Main Menu — Shows current period and unposted counts
3
Post all remaining transactions

Before closing the month, you must post ALL transactions. Navigate to:

Main tab → Processing → Post All Transactions

Click the "POST TRANSACTIONS" button. Wait for the confirmation. The unposted count must reach zero (0).

Screenshot: Post All Transactions — Click the button when unposted count is greater than zero
4
Back up your database (recommended)

Go to: File tab → Administration → Backup Database

Click "Backup Database" and wait for the file to download. Keep this file safe.

5
Open Month-End Processing

Navigate to: File tab → Administration → Month-End Processing

You will see a panel showing:

  • Period to Close — e.g., "March 2026"
  • Period Range — the date range
  • Unposted Transactions — must show 0 (green)
Screenshot: Month-End Processing page — Shows current period info and the step wizard
6
Confirm the period

Type the period you want to close in the format M/YYYY.

Example: To close March 2026, type: 3/2026

Then click "Confirm".

7
Execute the Month-End Close

Click the red "Close Period Now" button. A final confirmation will appear listing what will happen:

  • All account balances will be rolled forward
  • Transaction tables will be cleared for the new month
  • If December, income/expense accounts will be reset to zero

Click "OK" to proceed and wait for the processing to complete.

8
Done! You are now in the new month 🎉

You will see a green message: "Period Closed Successfully". The system automatically advances to the next month. You can start entering transactions for the new period!

🚫 Warning: This Cannot Be Undone!Once you close a month, you cannot go back. That is why Step 4 (Backup) is very important. If something goes wrong, contact your System Administrator to restore the backup.

Special Case: Year-End Close (December)

If the current month is December, the month-end close will also perform a Year-End Reset:

  • All income and expense accounts will be reset to zero
  • The system advances to January of the next year
  • A yellow warning banner will appear to alert you
⚠️ Extra Caution for DecemberMake sure all year-end adjustments are entered before closing December. Review your Income Statement and Balance Sheet reports.

Quick Reference: Navigation Map

ActionWhere to Find It
Enter TransactionsMain tab → Data Entry → choose type
Post TransactionsMain tab → Processing → Post All Transactions
Review ReportsQuery/Report tab → Report Generation
Backup DatabaseFile tab → Administration → Backup Database
Close the MonthFile tab → Administration → Month-End Processing
Check Current PeriodMain screen shows "Current Period: [Month Year]"

Troubleshooting

Q: "The system says I have unposted transactions."

Go to Main tab → Processing → Post All Transactions and click "POST TRANSACTIONS". Wait for it to finish, then go back to Month-End Processing.

Q: "I accidentally closed the wrong month!"

Month-end close cannot be reversed. If you have a backup, contact your System Administrator to restore it.

Q: "The screen is stuck on 'Processing…'"

This can take up to 2 minutes. Please wait. If it takes longer, refresh the page and check if the period has advanced.

Q: "I see a yellow warning about Year-End."

This is normal for December. It means income and expense accounts will be reset. Make sure all year-end adjustments are complete before proceeding.
